# learn-eval

> A Claude Code skill (MIT) that extracts reusable patterns from a session, self-evaluates their quality before saving, and decides the right save location (Global vs Project). Adds a checklist + holistic-judgment gate to the `/learn` flow so noise skills are dropped, absorbed, or improved before they enter the skill library.

## Core documentation

- [llms-full.txt](llms-full.txt): self-contained Q&A reference for AI agents (Project Facts, Prior Research, 9 question-form H2 sections)
- [README.md](README.md) / [README.ja.md](README.ja.md): human-facing narrative (English + Japanese)
- [SKILL.md](skills/learn-eval/SKILL.md): full skill specification — what to extract, save-location decision, quality gate, verdict flow

## Architecture

- 4 verdicts: **Save** (independent, specific, well-scoped), **Improve then Save** (worth keeping but needs revision), **Absorb into [X]** (existing skill should host this content), **Drop** (trivial, redundant, or abstract)
- Quality gate: deterministic checklist (skill grep, MEMORY.md dedup, existing-skill check, reusability) + holistic verdict
- Save locations: `~/.claude/skills/learned/` (global, cross-project) or `.claude/skills/learned/` (project-local); default to global when unsure
- Companion file: [skills/learn-eval/knowledge-placement-decision.md](skills/learn-eval/knowledge-placement-decision.md)

## Standard reference

- [Agent Skill specification](https://agentskills.io/specification): the Claude Code skill format used by this repo
- [SkillsMP marketplace](https://agentskills.io/specification): one-step install via `/skills add`

## Sibling skills

- [llms-txt-writer](https://github.com/shimo4228/llms-txt-writer): writes AI-facing docs optimized for ChatGPT / Perplexity / Gemini citation
- [skill-comply](https://github.com/shimo4228/skill-comply): measures whether agents actually follow skill / rule definitions
- [context-sync](https://github.com/shimo4228/context-sync): audits and fixes project documentation roles
- [search-first](https://github.com/shimo4228/search-first): research-before-coding workflow
- [skill-stocktake](https://github.com/shimo4228/skill-stocktake): quality audit for skills and commands
- [rules-distill](https://github.com/shimo4228/rules-distill): scan installed skills, extract cross-cutting principles, and distill them into rules
- [daily-research](https://github.com/shimo4228/daily-research): cron-driven daily research digest — theme selection, web research, LLM-as-Judge evaluation
